era: Hip-Hop. E=6 -- Tacoma hip-hop on Nastymix Records. Regional reach. Nastymix trilogy continues (177-178). Tacoma returns (first at 143). v1.49.318.

theory_nodes.concept: Regional reach as E=6 -- Tacoma hip-hop on a Seattle label. Nastymix credential. Pioneer generation. Group dynamics as multiplier. Regional touring building PNW hip-hop circuit.
examples: Group format: multiple MCs, collective energy.
theory_nodes.description: Regional reach. E=6 through Nastymix credential and Tacoma representation.

genealogy.relationship: High Performance at 178. Tacoma dimension of Nastymix.

theory_nodes.concept: Label as regional bridge -- Nastymix Records' geographic diversity (Seattle + Tacoma) transformed it from a local label into a regional platform. First label-based geographic bridge in the engine.
examples: Kid Sensation (Seattle) + High Performance (Tacoma) on same label.
theory_nodes.description: Label as bridge. Nastymix spans Seattle-Tacoma.

genealogy.relationship: High Performance at 178. Tacoma on Seattle infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: Tacoma returns -- second Tacoma entry (first at 143). The Tacoma-Seattle relationship mirrors broader PNW dynamics: proximity and distinction as productive cultural tension.
examples: Second entry at 178 through Nastymix Records.
Proximity and distinction as productive tension.
theory_nodes.description: Tacoma returns. Second entry. Seattle-Tacoma axis documented.

cross_references.relationship: NASTYMIX LABEL! Both on Nastymix Records. Kid Sensation from Seattle, High Performance from Tacoma. Same label, two cities.
NASTYMIX FOUNDER! Mix-a-Lot signed High Performance. Trilogy culminates at 179 with the label founder at E=7.
TACOMA RETURNS! First Tacoma entry at 143. High Performance at 178. Second appearance for the city.
REGIONAL! Blue Scholars documented Seattle. High Performance documented Tacoma. Two cities, one region, shared hip-hop tradition.
